Contact Profile: <From Bulldog> Costantini covers Washington news, especially the White House and Congress. "As a general assignment type of reporter, I have no specific agenda each day, but all of a sudden I might need to contact an expert," he says. "It helps when a PR person gets back to me quickly and gives me a sense of whether the potential interview is really going to be available. For us in radio, day-to-day news often rules out any kind of coverage of something that is not going to be the top story of the day." Additionally: "I find pitches from the more conservative think tanks in DC to be occasionally useful as a possible balance to what's coming from the biggest pitch machine in the nation"The White House," he says. "Some of them send out daily notes, based on what's in the news and the point-of-view we can expect from the experts available. But, I'm a day-to-day news person who often needs those things." Contact by email.

Contact Profile: <From Bulldog> Covers travel, health, nutrition, science, the environment, computers and entertainment. One minute of health and one minute of entertainment news are broadcast daily to affiliates in every major U.S. market. Howard says that 80% of the health material reported comes from universities and healthcare PR pros. However, she does not care for surveys or polls generated by universities or health institutions. Loves groundbreaking research. Press releases and short notes on upcoming health events and conferences are appreciated. Offer her solutions to commonplace health issues. Make sure guests speak clearly and concisely. "Guests with thick accents, I have to say no to most of the time," she says. Regarding entertainment news, Howard looks for "everything and anything." She insists that entertainers, ranging from musicians to artists to TV stars, be known personalities. She likes where-are-they-now stories as well. Press kits should be short and only include the highlights of a career. Pitches should either be health- or entertainment-oriented, not vague lifestyle-type stories. She asks that PR pros get right to the point and call her back the same day (when requested) with information in hand. Pitch a few weeks in advance of an event's opening.

Contact Profile: Allen Hunt is the newest edition to the WDBO talk line-up. He's a perfect fit for talk radio - he loves people, loves to talk and is full of opinions! His favorite things, which you'll hear him speak of often: God, family, and a joyful life including barbeque, bluegrass music, hiking, college sports and stock car racing.   Allen was a Methodist pastor for almost 20 years, spending 8 of those years at one of the largest congregations in the world. Prior to the ministry, Allen was a business man who had wandered away from God's plans. Allen found that a life serving himself did not result in much. One day, while entering a high-rise on Wall Street, Allen stepped over a homeless man trying to stay warm by lying on a subway grating. Right then, he felt that God sent him a mesasage to leave behind his plans and embrace God's plans. A life of serving others and following Jesus. Allen then became a minister and never looked back.
